Assembly: The assembly fTriLus1.hap1.1 is based on 38x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes 1 and 16 share a low level of homology and appear to be the sex chromosome pair. However, in the absence of concrete evidence, they have not been labelled as such. There is a large heterozygous inversion on Chromosome 24 from ~2.4 -6.5 Mbp.

Assembly: The assembly drChaAngu1.1 is based on 40x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative circular sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly of Prunus ramburei (drPruRamb1) is based on 42X PacBio data and 170X Omni-C Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 36 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 3.46 Mb (with the largest being 0.41 Mb). Additionally, 267 regions totaling 14.15 Mb (with the largest being 0.97 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial and chloroplastic genomes were assembled using OATK. For the mitochondrial genome, two circular forms were assembled and named MIT_1, MIT_2. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 3 haplotypic regions and 23 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 5.23 Mb and 0.60 Mb, respectively (with the largest being 3.57 Mb and 0.062 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Chromosomes 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 are assembled in a single contig, and chromosomes 1 and 2 are composed of two contigs. Chromosomes 1, 2, 3, 4, and 8 have telomeric repeats at both extremities, and chromosomes 5, 6, and 7 have them only at the beginning.

Assembly: On Nov 25, 2025 this sequence version replaced OZ207537.1.; The assembly of Macrothele cretica (qqMacCret1) is based on 19.7X PacBio data and 92X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 4 contigs were identified as contaminants bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 1.613 Mb (with the largest being 1.279 Mb). Additionally, 1161 regions totaling 239.165 Mb (with the largest being 8.15 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Sexual chromosomes renamed X1 to X11 according to length.

Assembly: The assembly ilOleArcu1.hap1.1 is based on 61x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. The order and orientation of scaffolds making up the W chromosomes is uncertain. Regions on chromosomes B1 (9.9 Mbp - 21.3 Mbp, 26.0 Mbp - 40.0 Mbp) and B2 (full length) are collapsed based on coverage, that suggests the potential presence of additional copies of these chromosomes. Chromosomes B1 B2 and B3 lack of ancestral BUSCO genes and have no alignment to closest relative genomes. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 19 (0 Mbp - 0.6 Mbp) and B1 (28.2 Mbp - 31.3 Mbp) are unknown.
